roadmap.title: 45-Day GenAI Engineer Roadmap
roadmap.description: A structured 45-day intermediate curriculum for practitioners who already know Python, ML, and transformer basics, focused on deep transformer internals, advanced fine-tuning (RLHF/DPO/PEFT), production RAG, agents, inference optimization, and scalable LLMOps to prepare for mid-level GenAI Engineer roles.
roadmap.outcome: Ability to design, optimize, fine-tune, evaluate, and deploy production-grade Generative AI systems at scale.

days.title: Transformer Internals Deep Dive
objectives: Master the full transformer forward pass mathematically
Understand residual streams and layer normalization placement
Analyze parameter and FLOP budgets per layer
days.intermediate_explanation: At the intermediate level you stop treating the transformer as a black box. The residual stream view treats each layer as reading from and writing to a shared communication channel, which explains why pre-norm architectures train more stably than the original post-norm design. You learn to count parameters and estimate FLOPs per token so you can reason about memory, latency, and cost before writing a single training job.
days.practical_task: Given a config (d_model, n_layers, n_heads, vocab), compute total parameters and per-token FLOPs by hand, then verify against a PyTorch model summary.
days.mini_project: Instrument nanoGPT to log per-layer activation norms and visualize how the residual stream evolves across layers for a sample input.
days.assignment: Write a technical report comparing pre-norm vs post-norm transformers, including a training-stability experiment on a tiny model.

quiz.question: The residual stream acts as a...
A transformer's forward-pass FLOPs scale roughly with...

youtube.title: Let's build GPT: from scratch, in code, spelled out

research_papers.title: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)
On Layer Normalization in the Transformer Architecture (Xiong et al., 2020)

days.expected_outcome: You can analyze a transformer's compute and memory profile and explain architectural design choices.

days.title: Attention Variants and Flash Attention
days.intermediate_explanation: The quadratic cost of attention dominates long-context inference. MQA and GQA reduce the number of key/value heads to shrink the KV cache, trading a little quality for large memory savings, which is why modern models like Llama use GQA. Flash Attention reorders the computation to be IO-aware, avoiding materializing the full attention matrix in HBM and delivering major speedups without approximation.
days.practical_task: Benchmark standard attention vs Flash Attention (via PyTorch SDPA) on increasing sequence lengths and plot latency and memory.
days.mini_project: Implement GQA in a small transformer and measure the KV cache reduction and quality impact versus MHA.
days.assignment: Write an analysis of why GQA is the industry default, including a KV-cache size calculation for a 7B model at 8k context.

quiz.question: Standard attention scales with sequence length as...

research_papers.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention (Dao et al., 2022)
GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models (Ainslie et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can choose and benchmark attention variants for efficient inference.

days.title: Positional Encodings: RoPE and ALiBi
objectives: Compare RoPE, ALiBi, and learned encodings
days.intermediate_explanation: Position encoding determines how well a model handles long context. RoPE encodes relative position by rotating query and key vectors, and it has become the standard because it extrapolates and can be scaled (via NTK-aware or YaRN methods) to extend context windows post-training. ALiBi biases attention scores by distance, offering strong length extrapolation with no learned parameters. Understanding these is key to long-context engineering.
days.practical_task: Implement RoPE in PyTorch and verify that relative position information is preserved under rotation.
days.mini_project: Take a small RoPE model and apply NTK-aware scaling to extend its usable context, measuring perplexity across lengths.
days.assignment: Write a comparison of RoPE vs ALiBi for long-context tasks with an experiment on extrapolation behavior.

quiz.question: YaRN and NTK scaling are used to...
ALiBi adds a bias based on...

research_papers.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding (Su et al., 2021)
YaRN: Efficient Context Window Extension of LLMs (Peng et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can implement and extend positional encodings for long-context models.

days.title: Advanced Tokenization and Vocabulary Design
days.intermediate_explanation: Tokenizer quality directly affects model performance and cost. Fertility (tokens per word) varies drastically across languages, penalizing under-represented ones. You learn to train tokenizers, measure compression ratios, and design vocabularies that balance sequence length against embedding table size. Correct chat templates and special tokens are essential for instruction-tuned models to behave properly.
days.practical_task: Train a BPE tokenizer on a domain corpus with the tokenizers library and compare its fertility to a general-purpose tokenizer.
days.mini_project: Build a tokenizer analysis dashboard that compares compression, fertility, and cost across models for multiple languages.
days.assignment: Analyze how tokenization affects arithmetic and code tasks, with concrete failure examples and explanations.

quiz.question: Chat templates define...
options: How roles/messages are formatted into tokens
quiz.answer: How roles/messages are formatted into tokens

research_papers.title: SentencePiece: A simple and language independent subword tokenizer (Kudo & Richardson, 2018)

days.expected_outcome: You can train and evaluate tokenizers and reason about their impact on models.

days.title: LLM Training Pipeline End-to-End
days.intermediate_explanation: Training LLMs reliably is an engineering discipline. Sequence packing maximizes GPU utilization, warmup plus cosine decay stabilizes early training, and gradient clipping prevents blow-ups. You learn to read training curves to catch loss spikes early, checkpoint safely for long runs, and log everything so experiments are reproducible and debuggable.
days.practical_task: Configure and launch a small language model pretraining run with warmup, cosine schedule, gradient clipping, and W&B logging.
days.mini_project: Pretrain a small GPT on a curated corpus, deliberately induce and then fix a loss spike, and document the diagnosis.
days.assignment: Produce a runbook for LLM training that covers hyperparameter selection, monitoring, and recovery from common failures.

research_papers.title: An Empirical Model of Large-Batch Training (McCandlish et al., 2018)

days.expected_outcome: You can configure, monitor, and debug an LLM training run end-to-end.

days.title: Scaling Laws and Compute-Optimal Training
days.intermediate_explanation: Scaling laws let you predict model performance from compute, parameters, and data before training. Chinchilla showed that many early models were undertrained, establishing roughly 20 tokens per parameter as compute-optimal. But because models are trained once and served millions of times, production teams often overtrain smaller models to reduce inference cost. Understanding these tradeoffs guides real budget decisions.
days.practical_task: Given a fixed compute budget, use scaling-law formulas to choose an optimal model size and token count.
days.mini_project: Fit a small-scale scaling curve by training several tiny models of increasing size and plotting loss vs compute.
days.assignment: Write a decision memo recommending model size and training tokens for a hypothetical product, justified with scaling laws.

quiz.question: Chinchilla's key finding was that many models were...
Overtraining a small model helps reduce...

youtube.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models Explained

research_papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models (Kaplan et al., 2020)
Training Compute-Optimal LLMs (Chinchilla, Hoffmann et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can size models and data budgets using scaling-law reasoning.

days.title: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
days.intermediate_explanation: Mixture-of-Experts models scale parameter count without proportionally scaling compute by activating only a few expert subnetworks per token. A router chooses top-k experts, and an auxiliary load-balancing loss prevents collapse where all tokens route to the same expert. MoE powers efficient frontier models like Mixtral, giving large model capacity at the inference cost of a much smaller dense model.
days.practical_task: Implement a simple top-2 MoE feedforward layer with a router and load-balancing loss, and inspect expert utilization.
days.mini_project: Convert a small dense transformer's MLP into an MoE layer and compare quality, active parameters, and throughput.
days.assignment: Write an analysis of MoE tradeoffs covering memory, routing instability, and serving challenges.

quiz.question: MoE activates...
options: A subset of experts per token
quiz.answer: A subset of experts per token

quiz.question: MoE increases parameters while keeping ______ roughly constant.

research_papers.title: Switch Transformers (Fedus et al., 2021)
Mixtral of Experts (Jiang et al., 2024)

days.expected_outcome: You understand and can implement basic MoE layers and their tradeoffs.

days.title: Data Curation and Preprocessing for LLMs
days.intermediate_explanation: Model quality is bounded by data quality. Curation involves aggressive deduplication (near-duplicates hurt generalization), quality filtering with classifiers, and decontamination against benchmarks to keep evaluations honest. Data mixture weights across domains materially change downstream abilities. PII and safety filtering are both ethical and legal requirements, making this a critical, often underrated, engineering skill.
days.practical_task: Build a data-cleaning pipeline that performs language detection, quality filtering, and MinHash deduplication on a raw text dump.
days.mini_project: Create a reproducible dataset preparation pipeline with a datasheet documenting sources, filters, and dedup statistics.
days.assignment: Design a data mixture for a coding-focused model and justify domain weights with expected effects.

research_papers.title: The RefinedWeb Dataset for Falcon LLM (Penedo et al., 2023)
Deduplicating Training Data Makes Language Models Better (Lee et al., 2021)

days.expected_outcome: You can build high-quality, decontaminated data pipelines for LLM training.

days.title: Distributed Training: DDP, FSDP, DeepSpeed
days.intermediate_explanation: Large models exceed single-GPU memory, so you shard the work. DDP replicates the model and splits data; FSDP and DeepSpeed ZeRO shard parameters, gradients, and optimizer states across devices to fit huge models. Tensor and pipeline parallelism split individual layers or stages. The engineering challenge is minimizing inter-GPU communication, which quickly becomes the bottleneck.
days.practical_task: Convert a single-GPU training script to multi-GPU using FSDP or DeepSpeed and measure throughput scaling.
days.mini_project: Fine-tune a model that does not fit on one GPU using ZeRO-3 offloading and document memory and speed tradeoffs.
days.assignment: Write a guide explaining when to use DDP vs FSDP vs tensor/pipeline parallelism with memory calculations.

quiz.question: The main cost of distributed training is...

research_papers.title: ZeRO: Memory Optimizations Toward Training Trillion Parameter Models (Rajbhandari et al., 2019)

days.expected_outcome: You can scale training across multiple GPUs using sharding frameworks.

days.title: Mixed Precision and Quantization Theory
days.intermediate_explanation: Numeric precision governs the memory-speed-accuracy tradeoff. BF16 has become the training default because its wide exponent range avoids the overflow issues of FP16 without needing loss scaling. Quantization compresses weights to INT8 or INT4 for cheaper inference, either post-training or via quantization-aware training. Understanding the format tradeoffs is essential before applying practical quantization tools.
days.practical_task: Train the same small model in FP32, FP16, and BF16, comparing memory, speed, and final loss.
days.mini_project: Quantize a model to INT8 with post-training quantization and measure accuracy loss versus speedup.
days.assignment: Write a technical explainer on why BF16 is preferred over FP16 for LLM training, with numeric examples.

quiz.question: BF16 is preferred over FP16 because it has...

research_papers.title: Mixed Precision Training (Micikevicius et al., 2017)
LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers (Dettmers et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You understand precision tradeoffs and can apply mixed-precision and basic quantization.

days.title: Fine-Tuning Strategies: Full vs PEFT
objectives: Choose the right method per task
days.intermediate_explanation: There is a spectrum of adaptation methods. Full fine-tuning maximizes capacity but is costly and risks catastrophic forgetting of general abilities. PEFT methods (adapters, prefix tuning, prompt tuning, LoRA) update tiny parameter subsets, are cheaper, and are easier to serve as swappable modules. Choosing correctly depends on data size, task divergence, and serving constraints.
days.practical_task: Fine-tune the same base model with full FT and with LoRA on one task, comparing quality, memory, and forgetting.
days.mini_project: Build a benchmark comparing three PEFT methods on a task, reporting a quality/cost frontier.
days.assignment: Create a decision framework for selecting a fine-tuning method given dataset size, budget, and deployment needs.

quiz.question: Catastrophic forgetting is when a model...
A serving advantage of adapters is...

research_papers.title: Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for NLP (Adapters, Houlsby et al., 2019)

days.expected_outcome: You can select and justify a fine-tuning strategy for any adaptation task.

days.title: LoRA and QLoRA in Depth
days.intermediate_explanation: LoRA approximates weight updates as the product of two low-rank matrices, dramatically cutting trainable parameters. Rank and alpha control capacity and effective learning rate, and choosing which modules to target (attention only vs all linear layers) affects quality. QLoRA loads the frozen base in NF4 4-bit precision, enabling fine-tuning of large models on a single GPU. Adapter merging and multi-adapter serving make this production-ready.
days.practical_task: Sweep LoRA rank and alpha on a task and plot how they affect quality and overfitting.
days.mini_project: Fine-tune a 7B+ model with QLoRA, merge the adapter, and benchmark the merged model against the base.
days.assignment: Document QLoRA best practices including target modules, rank selection, and memory budgeting for a given GPU.

quiz.question: QLoRA quantizes the base model to...

research_papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models (Hu et al., 2021)
QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs (Dettmers et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can expertly fine-tune large models with LoRA/QLoRA and tune their hyperparameters.

days.title: Instruction Tuning and Dataset Construction
topics: Data filtering and dedup for SFT
days.intermediate_explanation: Instruction tuning turns a base model into a helpful assistant using curated instruction/response pairs. Quality matters far more than quantity; a few thousand excellent examples often beat hundreds of thousands of noisy ones (the LIMA finding). Synthetic generation methods like Self-Instruct and Evol-Instruct scale data creation, and correct loss masking ensures the model learns responses, not prompts.
days.practical_task: Construct a small high-quality instruction dataset with correct loss masking and fine-tune a model on it.
days.mini_project: Use an LLM to generate a synthetic instruction dataset via Evol-Instruct, filter it, and fine-tune, comparing to a baseline.
days.assignment: Write a report on instruction data quality principles, referencing LIMA, with a curation checklist.

quiz.question: SFT loss masking ensures the model learns...

research_papers.title: LIMA: Less Is More for Alignment (Zhou et al., 2023)
Self-Instruct: Aligning LMs with Self-Generated Instructions (Wang et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can build high-quality instruction datasets and run effective SFT.

days.title: RLHF and PPO
days.intermediate_explanation: RLHF aligns models with human preferences in three stages: SFT, training a reward model on human preference comparisons, then optimizing the policy with PPO against that reward while a KL penalty keeps it close to the reference model. The main pitfalls are reward hacking and training instability, which is why simpler alternatives like DPO have gained popularity, but understanding PPO-based RLHF remains essential.
days.practical_task: Train a small reward model on a preference dataset and evaluate its agreement with held-out human preferences.
days.mini_project: Run a minimal RLHF loop with TRL (SFT to reward model to PPO) on a small model and observe reward and KL curves.
days.assignment: Write a technical explainer of the full RLHF pipeline including the role of the KL penalty and reward hacking risks.

quiz.question: The reward model is trained on...
The KL penalty in PPO keeps the policy...
Reward hacking is when the model...
options: Exploits the reward without true improvement
quiz.answer: Exploits the reward without true improvement

youtube.title: RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback

research_papers.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback (InstructGPT, Ouyang et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You understand and can run a basic RLHF pipeline with reward modeling and PPO.

days.title: DPO and Preference Optimization
days.intermediate_explanation: DPO reformulates preference alignment as a simple classification-style loss on chosen/rejected pairs, removing the need for a separate reward model and unstable RL loop. It is far easier to implement and tune, which is why many open models now use DPO or variants like ORPO (which merges SFT and preference tuning) and KTO (which needs only binary good/bad labels). This is the modern, practical alignment toolkit.
days.practical_task: Fine-tune a model with DPO on a preference dataset and compare it against the SFT baseline on quality.
days.mini_project: Run DPO and ORPO on the same data and compare training simplicity, stability, and output quality.
days.assignment: Write a comparison of DPO, PPO-RLHF, ORPO, and KTO covering data needs, stability, and use cases.

quiz.question: DPO removes the need for a separate...

research_papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization (Rafailov et al., 2023)
ORPO: Monolithic Preference Optimization without Reference Model (Hong et al., 2024)

days.expected_outcome: You can align models using DPO and modern preference-optimization methods.

days.title: LLM Evaluation and Benchmarks
days.intermediate_explanation: You cannot improve what you cannot measure. Standard benchmarks (MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval) give comparability, but they suffer from contamination and often do not reflect your real task. The professional approach combines harness-based benchmarks with custom, contamination-free evaluations for your domain, plus LLM-as-judge for open-ended quality, always with attention to statistical significance.
days.practical_task: Evaluate a model on a benchmark using the LM Evaluation Harness and interpret the results critically.
days.mini_project: Build a domain-specific evaluation suite (with a golden dataset and LLM-as-judge) and score two candidate models.
days.assignment: Write a critique of a popular benchmark, identifying contamination or validity issues and proposing improvements.

quiz.question: A key risk with public benchmarks is...

research_papers.title: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding (MMLU, Hendrycks et al., 2020)
Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench (Zheng et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can rigorously evaluate LLMs with standard and custom benchmarks.

days.title: Advanced Prompt Engineering and Optimization
days.intermediate_explanation: Beyond hand-crafted prompts, advanced techniques treat prompting as an optimizable program. Self-consistency samples multiple reasoning paths and votes, Tree-of-Thoughts explores branches, and reflection lets models critique and revise. Frameworks like DSPy compile and automatically optimize prompts against metrics, replacing brittle manual tuning with a systematic, measurable workflow.
days.practical_task: Implement self-consistency on a reasoning task and measure accuracy gains versus a single chain-of-thought.
days.mini_project: Use DSPy to build and automatically optimize a multi-step pipeline against a metric, comparing to manual prompts.
days.assignment: Write a comparison of self-consistency, ToT, and reflection with an experiment measuring cost vs accuracy tradeoffs.

quiz.question: Reflection lets a model...
options: Critique and revise its own output
quiz.answer: Critique and revise its own output

research_papers.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning (Wang et al., 2022)
Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with LLMs (Yao et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can systematically optimize prompts and reasoning pipelines.

days.title: Structured Output and Function Calling
days.intermediate_explanation: Reliable structured output is what turns an LLM into a dependable software component. Rather than hoping the model returns valid JSON, you enforce it with schema-guided generation, constrained decoding that restricts tokens to a grammar, and validation libraries like Pydantic, Instructor, and Outlines. Robust function calling with validation and retry logic is a core production skill.
days.practical_task: Use Instructor or Outlines to guarantee a Pydantic-validated JSON output from an LLM for an extraction task.
days.mini_project: Build a document-to-structured-data extractor with schema validation, constrained decoding, and automatic retry on failure.
days.assignment: Compare prompt-based JSON, JSON mode, and constrained decoding on reliability, measuring failure rates.

quiz.question: Constrained decoding restricts...
options: Which tokens can be generated to fit a grammar
quiz.answer: Which tokens can be generated to fit a grammar

quiz.question: Function calling lets a model return...

research_papers.title: Efficient Guided Generation for LLMs (Willard & Louf, 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can force reliable structured outputs from LLMs for production integration.

days.title: Advanced RAG Architectures
topics: Naive vs advanced vs modular RAG
days.intermediate_explanation: Advanced RAG treats retrieval as a modular, optimizable system. Pre-retrieval steps rewrite and route queries, post-retrieval steps compress and rerank context, and patterns like sentence-window and parent-document retrieval decouple the chunk you embed from the chunk you feed the model. Self-RAG and corrective RAG add self-assessment loops that decide when and what to retrieve, sharply reducing hallucinations.
days.practical_task: Implement parent-document retrieval and measure the answer-quality improvement over naive fixed-chunk RAG.
days.mini_project: Build a modular RAG system with query rewriting, reranking, and context compression, benchmarked with RAGAS.
days.assignment: Produce a RAG failure taxonomy for a real corpus and map each failure to a specific architectural fix.

quiz.question: Post-retrieval compression aims to...
options: Fit the most relevant context in the window
quiz.answer: Fit the most relevant context in the window

research_papers.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique (Asai et al., 2023)
Retrieval-Augmented Generation for LLMs: A Survey (Gao et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can design modular, high-accuracy RAG architectures.

days.title: Chunking and Indexing Strategies
days.intermediate_explanation: How you split and index documents often matters more than which model you use. Semantic chunking respects meaning boundaries, multi-vector indexing stores several representations per document (raw, summary, hypothetical questions), and hierarchical methods like RAPTOR build tree summaries for multi-level retrieval. Choosing the right strategy per document type is a high-leverage engineering decision.
days.practical_task: Compare fixed, recursive, and semantic chunking on the same corpus and measure retrieval quality for each.
days.mini_project: Implement a RAPTOR-style hierarchical index and compare it to flat chunking on multi-hop questions.
days.assignment: Write a chunking-strategy guide mapping document types (code, legal, chat logs) to recommended approaches.

research_papers.title: RAPTOR: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval (Sarthi et al., 2024)

days.expected_outcome: You can design optimal chunking and indexing for any document type.

days.title: Hybrid Search and Reranking
days.intermediate_explanation: Dense embeddings capture semantics but miss exact terms like IDs and error codes, where sparse BM25 excels. Hybrid search fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. A cross-encoder reranker then scores each query-document pair jointly for far higher precision than bi-encoders, at added latency. ColBERT's late interaction offers a middle ground. Tuning this stack is central to production retrieval quality.
days.practical_task: Build a hybrid retriever (BM25 + dense) with RRF and add a cross-encoder reranker, measuring nDCG improvements.
days.mini_project: Create a retrieval-quality benchmark comparing dense-only, hybrid, and hybrid+rerank on a labeled query set.
days.assignment: Analyze the latency vs quality tradeoff of reranking top-k values and recommend a production setting.

research_papers.title: ColBERT: Efficient and Effective Passage Search via Late Interaction (Khattab & Zaharia, 2020)

days.expected_outcome: You can build and tune hybrid retrieval with reranking for high precision.

days.title: Query Transformation Techniques
days.intermediate_explanation: User queries are often short and ambiguous, hurting retrieval. Query transformation fixes this before search: HyDE generates a hypothetical answer to embed instead of the raw query, multi-query and RAG-Fusion issue several reformulations and merge results, decomposition breaks complex questions into sub-questions, and semantic routing sends queries to the right index or tool. These techniques dramatically improve recall.
days.practical_task: Implement HyDE and multi-query retrieval and compare recall against direct query embedding.
days.mini_project: Build a query-transformation layer with routing and decomposition for a multi-source knowledge base.
days.assignment: Evaluate HyDE, multi-query, and decomposition on complex multi-hop questions and report which wins where.

quiz.question: Semantic routing directs a query to...

research_papers.title: Precise Zero-Shot Dense Retrieval without Relevance Labels (HyDE, Gao et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can boost retrieval recall with query transformation and routing.

days.title: Vector Database Internals and Optimization
days.intermediate_explanation: At scale, vector search becomes a systems problem. HNSW builds navigable small-world graphs tuned by parameters like M and ef, while IVF with product quantization compresses vectors to fit billions in memory. You learn to navigate the recall-latency-memory triangle, choose pre- vs post-filtering, and shard indexes, decisions that directly control cost and quality in production retrieval.
days.practical_task: Tune HNSW parameters (M, ef_construction, ef_search) on a dataset and plot the recall-latency tradeoff curve.
days.mini_project: Benchmark an IVF+PQ index vs HNSW at 1M+ vectors, comparing recall, latency, and memory footprint.
days.assignment: Write a tuning guide for a chosen vector DB covering index selection, parameters, and filtering strategies.

research_papers.title: Efficient and robust approximate nearest neighbor search using HNSW (Malkov & Yashunin, 2016)
Product Quantization for Nearest Neighbor Search (Jegou et al., 2011)

days.expected_outcome: You can tune and scale vector databases for production workloads.

days.title: GraphRAG and Knowledge Graphs
prerequisites: Day 22-23: Query transforms and vector DB internals
days.intermediate_explanation: Vector RAG struggles with global, connect-the-dots questions that span an entire corpus. GraphRAG extracts entities and relationships into a knowledge graph, clusters it into communities, and pre-summarizes each community, enabling both local (entity-centric) and global (thematic) queries. Combining graph traversal with vector retrieval gives the best of structured and semantic search.
days.practical_task: Extract entities and relations from a document set with an LLM and build a small knowledge graph you can query.
days.mini_project: Build a GraphRAG pipeline over a corpus and compare its global-question answers against vector RAG.
days.assignment: Write an analysis of when GraphRAG outperforms vector RAG, with example query types for each.

research_papers.title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization (Edge et al., 2024)

days.expected_outcome: You can build GraphRAG systems for global, relationship-aware queries.

days.title: Agentic RAG
days.intermediate_explanation: Agentic RAG replaces a fixed retrieve-then-generate pipeline with an agent that decides whether, what, and how many times to retrieve. It can grade retrieved documents, rewrite the query and retry when results are poor, and combine multiple sources, implementing corrective and self-RAG patterns as a control loop. This adaptivity is the current state of the art for complex knowledge tasks.
days.practical_task: Build a LangGraph agent that retrieves, grades relevance, and re-queries when documents are insufficient.
days.mini_project: Create an agentic RAG assistant with document grading, query rewriting, and web-search fallback.
days.assignment: Compare static RAG vs agentic RAG on hard queries and quantify accuracy, latency, and cost differences.

quiz.question: Agentic RAG treats retrieval as...
options: A tool the agent decides to use
quiz.answer: A tool the agent decides to use

quiz.question: Document grading lets the agent...
options: Decide if retrieved context is relevant
quiz.answer: Decide if retrieved context is relevant

quiz.question: A tradeoff of agentic RAG is...

research_papers.title: Corrective Retrieval Augmented Generation (Yan et al., 2024)

days.expected_outcome: You can build adaptive, self-correcting agentic RAG systems.

days.title: Agent Frameworks Deep Dive (LangGraph)
days.intermediate_explanation: Production agents need explicit, debuggable control flow, not opaque loops. LangGraph models agents as state machines: nodes perform work, conditional edges route based on state, and cycles enable iteration. Built-in checkpointing gives durable memory and time-travel debugging, while human-in-the-loop breakpoints let you approve or edit actions before they execute, essential for safe, reliable agents.
days.practical_task: Build a LangGraph agent with conditional routing, a cycle for retries, and a human-approval breakpoint.
days.mini_project: Create a stateful multi-tool agent with persistence and streaming that a user can pause, inspect, and resume.
days.assignment: Design and diagram the state graph for a real agent use case and justify each node and edge.

research_papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models (Yao et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can build robust, stateful agents with explicit control flow.

days.title: Multi-Agent Systems
days.intermediate_explanation: Complex tasks benefit from teams of specialized agents. A supervisor pattern routes subtasks to expert agents (researcher, coder, reviewer) and integrates results, while hierarchical and network topologies enable richer collaboration. The tradeoff is cost and latency, so you learn when a single strong agent beats a crowd and how to control communication to avoid runaway loops.
days.practical_task: Build a supervisor multi-agent system where a coordinator delegates to a researcher and a writer agent.
days.mini_project: Create a multi-agent content pipeline (research, draft, critique, revise) and compare quality to a single agent.
days.assignment: Write a decision guide for single-agent vs multi-agent designs, including cost and reliability considerations.

quiz.question: The supervisor pattern is used to...
A key cost of multi-agent systems is...

research_papers.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation (Wu et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can design and coordinate multi-agent systems for complex tasks.

days.title: Agent Memory and Planning
days.intermediate_explanation: Capable agents need memory and planning. Short-term memory holds the current task, while long-term memory (episodic experiences and semantic facts) persists across sessions via a store the agent retrieves from. Planning decomposes goals into steps, and reflection lets agents learn from past attempts. Managing what stays in the finite context window versus external memory is the core engineering challenge.
days.practical_task: Add long-term memory to an agent using a vector store, with retrieval and periodic summarization.
days.mini_project: Build a personal-assistant agent that remembers user preferences across sessions and plans multi-step tasks.
days.assignment: Design a memory architecture for a long-running agent, specifying what is stored, retrieved, and forgotten.

quiz.question: Context window management decides what stays...
options: In the finite prompt vs external memory
quiz.answer: In the finite prompt vs external memory

research_papers.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior (Park et al., 2023)
Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning (Shinn et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can build agents with persistent memory and planning capabilities.

days.title: Tool Use and the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
days.intermediate_explanation: The Model Context Protocol standardizes how LLM applications connect to tools and data sources, so a tool built once (an MCP server) works across many clients. You learn to write clear tool schemas the model reliably invokes, handle parallel calls and errors gracefully, and sandbox tool execution for security. MCP is rapidly becoming the industry integration standard.
days.practical_task: Build a simple MCP server exposing two tools and connect it to an MCP-capable client to invoke them.
days.mini_project: Create a reusable MCP server (e.g., database + file access) with validation and sandboxing, usable by any agent.
days.assignment: Write a security analysis of tool execution risks and the mitigations you would apply in production.

quiz.question: Good tool descriptions primarily improve...
options: Whether the model calls the tool correctly
quiz.answer: Whether the model calls the tool correctly

research_papers.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools (Schick et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can build robust, reusable tool integrations using MCP.

days.title: LLM Orchestration and Workflows
days.intermediate_explanation: Not every task needs a fully autonomous agent. Anthropic's guidance distinguishes predictable workflows (chaining, routing, parallelization, evaluator-optimizer) from agents that decide their own path. Workflows are more reliable and cheaper when the steps are known, while agents suit open-ended problems. Choosing correctly, and making execution durable, is a hallmark of mature GenAI engineering.
days.practical_task: Implement three workflow patterns (chaining, routing, parallelization) for a task and compare reliability and cost.
days.mini_project: Build an evaluator-optimizer workflow that iteratively drafts and refines output until it passes a quality bar.
days.assignment: Write a guide for choosing workflows vs agents with a decision tree and cost/reliability analysis.

quiz.question: Workflows are preferred when steps are...
An evaluator-optimizer loop...
options: Iteratively refines output against a check
quiz.answer: Iteratively refines output against a check

research_papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models (Yao et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can architect reliable LLM workflows and know when to use agents.

days.title: LLM Inference Optimization (vLLM, TGI)
days.intermediate_explanation: Serving LLMs efficiently is a specialized discipline. Inference has a compute-bound prefill phase and a memory-bandwidth-bound decode phase. vLLM's PagedAttention manages the KV cache like OS virtual memory to eliminate fragmentation, and continuous batching keeps the GPU busy by swapping requests in and out per token. These techniques deliver order-of-magnitude throughput gains over naive serving.
days.practical_task: Serve a model with vLLM and benchmark throughput and latency against a naive Hugging Face generate loop.
days.mini_project: Deploy an OpenAI-compatible vLLM endpoint and load-test it, tuning batch and concurrency for best throughput.
days.assignment: Write an explainer on why PagedAttention and continuous batching improve throughput, with measured results.

quiz.question: The decode phase is bound by...

research_papers.title: Efficient Memory Management for LLM Serving with PagedAttention (Kwon et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can serve LLMs at high throughput using modern inference engines.

days.title: KV Cache and Speculative Decoding
days.intermediate_explanation: The KV cache stores past keys/values so each new token avoids recomputation, but it grows with context and dominates memory. You reduce it via GQA and cache quantization, and reuse it across requests with prefix caching. Speculative decoding uses a small draft model to propose several tokens that the large model verifies in parallel, cutting latency substantially without changing outputs.
days.practical_task: Enable prefix caching and speculative decoding in a serving engine and measure latency reduction.
days.mini_project: Benchmark speculative decoding with different draft models and quantify the accepted-token rate and speedup.
days.assignment: Write an analysis of KV-cache memory scaling with context and batch, and strategies to bound it.

quiz.question: Speculative decoding uses a draft model to...
options: Propose tokens verified by the big model
quiz.answer: Propose tokens verified by the big model

research_papers.title: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding (Leviathan et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can minimize inference latency with cache and decoding optimizations.

days.title: Model Quantization in Practice (GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF)
days.intermediate_explanation: Practical quantization lets you run large models on modest hardware. GPTQ and AWQ are calibration-based methods that preserve accuracy well at 4-bit for GPU inference, while GGUF (with llama.cpp) targets efficient CPU and edge deployment. You learn which format fits which deployment, how calibration data affects quality, and how to measure the accuracy cost of compression.
days.practical_task: Quantize a model with AWQ and GGUF, then compare output quality, size, and inference speed across formats.
days.mini_project: Build a quantization comparison report for a model across GPTQ/AWQ/GGUF at multiple bit widths with benchmarks.
days.assignment: Recommend a quantization strategy for three deployment targets (GPU server, laptop, edge) with justification.

youtube.title: GPTQ vs AWQ vs GGUF Quantization

research_papers.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Transformers (Frantar et al., 2022)
AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization (Lin et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can quantize and deploy models efficiently across hardware targets.

days.title: Serving and Scaling LLMs in Production
days.intermediate_explanation: Production serving must handle bursty, concurrent traffic within latency SLAs and a budget. You design a stack with load balancing, request queuing, and GPU autoscaling, route requests to the cheapest capable model, and add semantic and prompt caches to cut cost. The goal is minimizing cost per token while meeting p95 latency and reliability targets.
days.practical_task: Deploy a serving stack with a load balancer and a request queue, then load-test it to find its throughput ceiling.
days.mini_project: Build a multi-model gateway that routes queries to small/large models by difficulty and tracks cost per request.
days.assignment: Design a serving architecture for a target QPS and latency SLA, with a capacity and cost estimate.

quiz.question: A multi-model router saves cost by...
options: Routing easy queries to cheaper models
quiz.answer: Routing easy queries to cheaper models

quiz.question: Semantic caching returns cached answers for...

research_papers.title: Orca: A Distributed Serving System for Transformer-Based Generative Models (Yu et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can architect cost-efficient, scalable LLM serving systems.

days.title: LLMOps and Observability
days.intermediate_explanation: Mature LLMOps brings observability and controlled change to LLM systems. Distributed tracing reconstructs every step of a request for debugging, dashboards track latency/cost/quality, and prompt/model versioning plus A/B tests and canary releases let you ship changes safely. Capturing user feedback and detecting distribution drift close the loop for continuous improvement.
days.practical_task: Instrument a RAG or agent app with full tracing and a dashboard for latency, cost, and quality signals.
days.mini_project: Set up A/B testing between two prompt versions with online metrics and a statistically sound comparison.
days.assignment: Design an LLMOps observability plan covering tracing, versioning, alerts, and drift detection for a product.

quiz.question: Distributed tracing helps you...
options: Reconstruct and debug each request step
quiz.answer: Reconstruct and debug each request step

quiz.question: Canary releases reduce risk by...
options: Shipping to all users at once
Rolling out to a small subset first
quiz.answer: Rolling out to a small subset first

research_papers.title: Challenges in Deploying Machine Learning: a Survey of Case Studies (Paleyes et al., 2020)

days.expected_outcome: You can operate LLM systems with full observability and safe change management.

days.title: Evaluation and Testing Pipelines
days.intermediate_explanation: Shipping GenAI safely requires treating evaluation like software testing. You build offline eval suites (faithfulness, context relevance, answer correctness for RAG; trajectory correctness for agents), automate LLM-as-judge scoring, and add regression tests so a prompt or model change cannot silently degrade quality. Wiring these into CI/CD makes quality a gate, not an afterthought.
days.practical_task: Build an automated RAG evaluation pipeline with RAGAS metrics and a golden dataset, run on every change.
days.mini_project: Add an eval gate to a CI pipeline that blocks merges when quality metrics regress beyond a threshold.
days.assignment: Design an evaluation strategy document covering offline, online, and regression testing for a GenAI product.

quiz.question: Faithfulness measures whether an answer is...
Agent evaluation often checks the...
options: Trajectory of steps and tool calls
quiz.answer: Trajectory of steps and tool calls

research_papers.title: RAGAS: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation (Es et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can build automated evaluation pipelines integrated into CI/CD.

days.title: Guardrails, Safety, and Red-Teaming
days.intermediate_explanation: Any public GenAI system will be attacked. Prompt injection can hijack instructions, jailbreaks bypass safety, and tool-using agents create data-exfiltration risks. Defense in depth layers input validation, output filtering, least-privilege tool permissions, and monitoring. Systematic red-teaming, probing your own system adversarially, is how you find weaknesses before attackers do.
days.practical_task: Build a guardrail layer that detects prompt injection and moderates outputs, then try to bypass it.
days.mini_project: Red-team your own RAG/agent app with an attack suite and produce a findings report with mitigations.
days.assignment: Write a threat model for a tool-using agent covering injection, exfiltration, and privilege escalation with defenses.

research_papers.title: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models (Zou et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can secure and red-team GenAI systems with layered defenses.

days.title: Fine-Tuning Vision-Language Models
days.intermediate_explanation: Vision-language models pair a vision encoder with an LLM through a projection layer, letting text models reason over images. Fine-tuning them with visual instruction data (the LLaVA recipe) adapts them to domains like document, chart, or medical-image understanding. You typically freeze most of the model and train the projector and LoRA adapters, applying the fine-tuning skills you already have to multimodal data.
days.practical_task: Fine-tune a small VLM with LoRA on a visual-instruction dataset for a specific image-understanding task.
days.mini_project: Build a document-understanding assistant by fine-tuning a VLM to extract structured data from images.
days.assignment: Write an explainer of VLM architecture and a plan for fine-tuning one on a chosen visual domain.

quiz.question: A VLM connects a vision encoder to an LLM via a...
When fine-tuning VLMs you often freeze...
options: Most of the model, training projector/adapters
quiz.answer: Most of the model, training projector/adapters

research_papers.title: Visual Instruction Tuning (LLaVA, Liu et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can fine-tune vision-language models for domain-specific multimodal tasks.

days.title: Advanced Diffusion Models (ControlNet, LoRA)
days.intermediate_explanation: Advanced image generation is about control. Latent diffusion denoises in a compressed VAE space guided by a U-Net; different samplers trade speed for quality. ControlNet conditions generation on structure (pose, depth, edges), while LoRA and DreamBooth teach new styles or subjects with a handful of images. IP-Adapter enables image prompting. Together these give production-grade, controllable generation.
days.practical_task: Use ControlNet to generate images conditioned on a pose or edge map and compare samplers for quality/speed.
days.mini_project: Train a style or subject LoRA for Stable Diffusion and build a controllable generation pipeline around it.
days.assignment: Write a guide on controllable diffusion covering ControlNet, LoRA, and IP-Adapter with example use cases.

quiz.question: Latent diffusion denoises in the space of a...
DreamBooth is used to...
options: Teach a model a new subject/style
quiz.answer: Teach a model a new subject/style

research_papers.title: Adding Conditional Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models (ControlNet, Zhang et al., 2023)
High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models (Rombach et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can build controllable, customized image-generation pipelines.

days.title: Audio and Speech Generative AI
days.intermediate_explanation: Voice is a fast-growing GenAI interface. Whisper provides robust multilingual speech-to-text, modern TTS models produce natural speech and can clone voices, and audio-native models tokenize sound directly. Building a voice agent means orchestrating ASR to LLM to TTS with low latency and streaming, a highly marketable applied skill as voice assistants proliferate.
days.practical_task: Build an ASR-to-LLM-to-TTS pipeline that transcribes speech, answers with an LLM, and speaks the response.
days.mini_project: Create a real-time voice assistant with streaming transcription, LLM reasoning, and low-latency TTS.
days.assignment: Analyze latency sources in a voice pipeline and propose optimizations to reach conversational latency.

quiz.question: The main UX challenge for voice agents is...

research_papers.title: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision (Whisper, Radford et al., 2022)

days.expected_outcome: You can build real-time voice-enabled GenAI applications.

days.title: Cost Optimization and Caching Strategies
days.intermediate_explanation: GenAI costs scale with tokens and can spiral fast. You learn to profile cost drivers, then attack them: semantic caching serves near-duplicate queries for free, prompt caching discounts repeated context, model cascades try cheap models first and escalate only when needed, and batching plus context compression cut token spend. Disciplined cost engineering often reduces bills by an order of magnitude.
days.practical_task: Add semantic caching and prompt caching to an app and measure the resulting cost reduction on realistic traffic.
days.mini_project: Build a cost-optimized inference layer with a model cascade and caching, reporting cost-per-query before and after.
days.assignment: Audit a hypothetical GenAI product's cost structure and propose a prioritized optimization plan with estimates.

quiz.question: A model cascade tries...
options: Cheaper models first, escalating if needed
quiz.answer: Cheaper models first, escalating if needed

research_papers.title: FrugalGPT: How to Use LLMs While Reducing Cost and Improving Performance (Chen et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You can dramatically reduce the operating cost of GenAI systems.

days.title: Security and Data Privacy for GenAI
days.intermediate_explanation: Enterprises will not deploy GenAI that leaks data or breaks compliance. You learn to detect and redact PII before it reaches a model or logs, enforce per-tenant access control in RAG so users only retrieve permitted documents, and manage supply-chain risks from untrusted models and prompts. Audit logging and data-handling controls turn a prototype into something legal and security teams will approve.
days.practical_task: Add PII detection and redaction to an ingestion and logging pipeline and verify sensitive data never leaks.
days.mini_project: Build a multi-tenant RAG system with document-level access control so users only see permitted content.
days.assignment: Write a data-handling and compliance plan for a GenAI product covering PII, retention, and access control.

research_papers.title: Extracting Training Data from Large Language Models (Carlini et al., 2020)

days.expected_outcome: You can build compliant, privacy-preserving GenAI systems.

days.title: Advanced Deployment: Kubernetes and Autoscaling
days.intermediate_explanation: Production GenAI runs on orchestrated infrastructure. You containerize GPU workloads, schedule them on Kubernetes with GPU node pools, and autoscale on queue depth or latency using KEDA. Blue-green and canary deployment strategies enable zero-downtime, low-risk releases, and infrastructure as code makes the whole stack reproducible. These platform skills are what separate a demo from a real service.
days.practical_task: Containerize a vLLM service and deploy it to Kubernetes with a GPU node pool and an autoscaler.
days.mini_project: Build a full CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, evaluates, and canary-deploys a GenAI service to Kubernetes.
days.assignment: Design a production infrastructure diagram (with IaC) for a GenAI service meeting an availability SLA.

quiz.question: Canary deployment reduces risk by...
options: Releasing to a small percentage first
quiz.answer: Releasing to a small percentage first

research_papers.title: Borg, Omega, and Kubernetes (Burns et al., 2016)

days.expected_outcome: You can deploy and autoscale GenAI services on production-grade infrastructure.

days.title: Capstone Part 1: Architect a Production GenAI System
days.intermediate_explanation: The capstone integrates the intermediate curriculum into one production-grade system. You define real requirements and SLAs, design an architecture combining advanced RAG and/or agents with an optimized or fine-tuned model, and implement the core with evaluation and observability built in from the start. This mirrors exactly what a mid-level GenAI engineer delivers on the job.
days.practical_task: Write a system design doc (requirements, architecture, data flow, SLAs, cost model) and scaffold the repository.
days.mini_project: Build the core of a production GenAI application, for example an enterprise knowledge platform with agentic RAG.
days.assignment: Implement the core pipeline with an evaluation harness and tracing wired in, under version control.

quiz.question: A production system design should start with...
Evaluation and observability should be added...

research_papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for LLMs: A Survey (Gao et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You have designed and built the core of a production-grade GenAI system.

days.title: Capstone Part 2: Deploy, Optimize, and Interview Prep
days.intermediate_explanation: You finish and harden the capstone, then convert it into interview leverage. Deploy with an optimized serving stack, tune cost and latency, add guardrails and security, and produce a final evaluation report proving quality. Then rehearse GenAI system-design interviews, where you must reason aloud about RAG vs fine-tuning, scaling, evaluation, cost, and safety tradeoffs, the exact skills this roadmap built.
days.practical_task: Deploy the capstone with optimized serving, guardrails, and monitoring, then produce a final evaluation report.
days.mini_project: Ship the complete production GenAI system with a live demo, architecture docs, and a metrics dashboard.
days.assignment: Complete two GenAI system-design mock interviews (RAG platform and agent system) and write up your solutions.

quiz.question: A GenAI system-design interview tests reasoning about...
options: RAG/fine-tuning, scaling, eval, cost, safety tradeoffs
quiz.answer: RAG/fine-tuning, scaling, eval, cost, safety tradeoffs

quiz.question: A final evaluation report should demonstrate...
Before a public launch you must add...

research_papers.title: A Survey of Large Language Models (Zhao et al., 2023)

days.expected_outcome: You have a deployed, optimized production capstone and are ready for mid-level GenAI Engineer interviews.
